title: Yukawa-Screened Bose-Star Condensation
abstract: We study Bose-star formation in a Yukawa-Schrödinger-Poisson (YSP) system. A finite interaction range suppresses the infrared kinetic relaxation responsible for Bose-star condensation, modifying both the equilibrium Bose-star structure and the condensation timescale. We derive a screened kinetic condensation formula in which the ordinary gravitational Coulomb logarithm is replaced by a finite Yukawa transport logarithm. Static YSP solutions show that Yukawa screening broadens the Bose-star density profile relative to the ordinary Newtonian soliton. Fully dynamical pseudospectral simulations with homogeneous and isotropic initial conditions demonstrate that Yukawa screening systematically delays Bose-star condensation, in good agreement with the screened kinetic prediction after fitting a single overall normalization parameter.
summary: This paper explores how Yukawa-type screened interactions affect the formation of Bose-stars. It demonstrates that a finite interaction range delays the condensation process and results in less compact, broader density profiles. The findings are supported by both analytical kinetic theory and numerical simulations.
main_problem: Investigating how finite-range Yukawa interactions modify the formation, internal structure, and condensation timescale of Bose-stars compared to the standard long-range Newtonian case.
main_result: Yukawa screening broadens the Bose-star density profile and systematically delays the condensation timescale by suppressing infrared kinetic relaxation.
method: The study uses analytical derivation of a screened kinetic condensation formula and fully dynamical pseudospectral simulations with imaginary-time relaxation for ground-state profiles.
model_or_system: A Yukawa-Schrödinger-Poisson (YSP) system representing a self-gravitating bosonic gas (such as axion dark matter) with a screened gravitational potential.
key_observables: Bose-star density profiles, condensation timescale, and projected density field snapshots.
important_parameters: Yukawa screening mass (mu_Y), boson mass (m), and the dimensionless screening parameter (e*mu_Y).
main_assumptions: The Yukawa interaction is treated phenomenologically as a screened gravitational potential; the system is studied in a periodic box with homogeneous and isotropic initial conditions.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the broadening of static Bose-star profiles with increasing screening; Figure 2 displays density field snapshots demonstrating delayed formation; Figure 3 shows the evolution of density profiles; Figure 4 shows the evolution of maximum density; Figure 5 compares predicted vs. measured condensation times.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the YSP model, derives an analytical formula for screened kinetic condensation, presents static solutions for Bose-star profiles, details dynamical pseudospectral simulations, and concludes by validating the analytical predictions against numerical results.
why_interesting: This work provides insights into how modifying the range of interactions affects the relaxation dynamics and structural stability of many-body bosonic systems, which is highly relevant to the study of non-equilibrium dynamics in ultracold gases.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Static YSP Bose-star profiles obtained by imaginary- time relaxation for different Yukawa masses eµY . The profiles are normalized to the same central density to illustrate how Yukawa screening modifies the soliton structure. The case eµY = 0 corresponds to the ordinary Schr¨odinger-Poisson soli- ton.

FIG. 2. Snapshots of the projected density field for simula- tions with eL = 30 and f M = 20. The top row corresponds to the ordinary Schr¨odinger-Poisson case with eµY = 0, while the bottom row shows the Yukawa-screened evolution with eµY = 0.20. From left to right, the snapshots are taken at et = 0, et = 2200, and et = 3750, respectively.

FIG. 3. Spherically averaged density profiles for the simula- tion with eL = 30, f M = 20, and eµY = 0.20. Colored markers show density profiles extracted from simulations at different evolution times, et = 1500, 2200, and 3750. Solid curves denote the corresponding fitted static YSP ground-state solutions.

FIG. 5. Comparison between the predicted condensation times and the measured simulation results for Yukawa- screened runs. The prediction is computed using Eq. 13 with a single fitted normalization coefficient. The solid black line denotes perfect agreement. Different colors correspond to dif- ferent box sizes eL.
FIG. 4. Evolution of the maximum density for simulations with box size eL = 30, total mass f M = 20, and different Yukawa screening mass eµY .
